vulnerabilities.title: Complete Absence of Authentication System
vulnerabilities.description: The application implements zero authentication mechanisms. No login endpoints, credential validation, session management, or user identification exists. Every endpoint is publicly accessible without any user verification.

technical_details.token_systems: NONE (no JWT, OAuth, or API keys)
technical_details.evidence: Grep search for 'session_start|login|authenticate|$_SESSION' returned zero matches in application code

vulnerabilities.impact: Any anonymous internet user can access all application functionality without providing credentials. The SSTI vulnerability at POST /index.php is immediately exploitable without authentication bypass.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario: Attacker sends HTTP POST to /index.php with SSTI payload in 'name' parameter, achieving RCE without any authentication requirement
references: OWASP Top 10 2021 - A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures

vulnerabilities.title: Complete Absence of Authorization Controls
vulnerabilities.description: The application implements zero authorization checks. No role-based access control (RBAC), permission validation, access control lists (ACLs), or privilege verification exists anywhere in the codebase.

technical_details.code_evidence: Line 55: if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'POST') - Only checks HTTP method, not user permissions

vulnerabilities.impact: All users have identical access to all functionality. No privilege separation or access control exists. Everyone has maximum privileges by default.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario: Attacker directly accesses vulnerable endpoint without needing privilege escalation or authorization bypass
references: OWASP Top 10 2021 - A01:2021 Broken Access Control

vulnerabilities.title: No Session Management Implementation
vulnerabilities.description: The application implements zero session management. No calls to session_start(), no $_SESSION usage, no session cookies, and no state tracking between requests.

technical_details.impact_on_authz: Cannot track user identity or login state

vulnerabilities.impact: Application cannot maintain user state, implement authentication flows, or track user sessions. Each request is processed independently without context.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario: Session-based attacks (fixation, hijacking) are not applicable, but authentication is also impossible to implement

vulnerabilities.title: No Object Ownership Validation
vulnerabilities.description: While traditional IDOR vulnerabilities don't apply (no object ID parameters exist), the application demonstrates complete absence of object ownership validation patterns that would be required in a multi-user application.

technical_details.idor_risk: N/A (no persistent objects or multi-user architecture)
technical_details.parameter_inventory: Only 'name' parameter (free-text string, not object ID)

vulnerabilities.impact: Not directly exploitable in current implementation due to single-user, stateless design. Documents architectural gap for education.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario: N/A - traditional IDOR not applicable; SSTI is primary attack vector
references: OWASP Top 10 2021 - A01:2021 Broken Access Control (IDOR subcategory)

vulnerabilities.title: No Role Hierarchy or Privilege Levels
vulnerabilities.description: The application defines zero user roles (admin, user, guest, etc.) and has no privilege hierarchy. All users have identical access with no differentiation.

technical_details.code_search_evidence: Grep for 'role|ROLE_|admin|permission' returned zero matches in application code

vulnerabilities.impact: Cannot implement least privilege principle. All users have maximum access by default. No ability to restrict sensitive operations to privileged users.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario: Privilege escalation not required - everyone already has maximum privileges

vulnerabilities.title: No Authorization Middleware or Guards
vulnerabilities.description: The application has zero authorization middleware, decorators, or guards. No request pipeline exists to enforce access control before reaching vulnerable endpoints.
vulnerabilities.impact: No defense-in-depth. Requests reach vulnerable code without passing through any authorization decision points.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario: Attacker requests bypass any authorization checks because none exist in the request flow

vulnerabilities.title: No Cookie Security Attributes
vulnerabilities.description: While the application doesn't set cookies itself, it demonstrates complete absence of cookie security best practices. No HttpOnly, Secure, or SameSite attributes configured.
vulnerabilities.impact: If sessions were implemented, cookies would lack security flags, enabling XSS-based theft and CSRF attacks.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario: N/A in current implementation (no cookies set)
references: OWASP Session Management Cheat Sheet - Cookie Attributes

vulnerabilities.title: No Token-Based Authentication
vulnerabilities.description: The application implements zero token-based authentication mechanisms. No JWT validation, OAuth flows, API key verification, or Bearer token handling exists.
vulnerabilities.impact: Cannot implement modern API authentication patterns. All endpoints remain publicly accessible.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario: N/A - no tokens to steal or forge because none are used

vulnerabilities.title: No Apache Authorization Directives
vulnerabilities.description: Apache configuration contains zero authorization directives. No <Directory> access controls, Require directives, or Allow/Deny rules implemented.

technical_details.require_directives: NONE (no Require valid-user, Require ip, etc.)

vulnerabilities.impact: Web server grants public access to all files in DocumentRoot without restriction.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario: Attacker can access any file in /var/www/html without Apache-level authorization checks
references: Apache HTTP Server Access Control Documentation

vulnerabilities.title: Unauthenticated SSTI Endpoint Enables Direct RCE
vulnerabilities.description: The combination of zero authentication/authorization controls with a Server-Side Template Injection vulnerability creates a direct path to Remote Code Execution for any anonymous attacker.

technical_details.ssti_vulnerability: User input concatenated into Twig template (line 56)

vulnerabilities.impact: Complete container compromise achievable by unauthenticated attackers. Flag file accessible without authentication bypass or privilege escalation.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario:
1. Attacker sends POST to /index.php with SSTI payload in 'name' parameter
2. Twig renders malicious template (no Sandbox protection)
3. Attacker executes arbitrary system commands
4. Reads /flag.txt or achieves full RCE
5. No authentication/authorization checks prevent any step
vulnerabilities.remediation_notes: N/A - deliberately vulnerable CTF application. Secure implementation would require: (1) Authentication before endpoint access, (2) Authorization checks for template operations, (3) Twig Sandbox enforcement, (4) Input validation preventing template syntax
references: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code
